item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this a lot. Well-written and a good concept. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure what these other commenters are talking about (though it's been about a year since any comment was last made, maybe it's been rewritten in the meantime?) this actually struck me as quite a sad little story.

Those idiots accidentally killed the fly pope! Just tragic.

+1

item.title: Re: Nice -J, Kid
item.content:encoded: THAT'S NOT A VALID EMAIL. There is no actual foundation email, thank goodness.

item.title: Nice -J, Kid
item.content:encoded:
-1

Also linking to your own E-mail seems really unprofessional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm just happy that I'm not the only one who thinks of a fly when I hear the name Beelzebub.

item.title: Maybe better as a -J?
item.content:encoded: I say that this SCP would work better as a -J SCP. Either go all the way with being funny or all the way with being serious. I still upvoted, but please consider this in the future.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: An_Person, instead of replying to your own comments, please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kinda… it's really short and has some buildup but no payoff. It's basically a fly that's a Catholic priest or something similar, no? I think it can be expanded, as it stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was…very brief, and not very well put together in my opinion. There was a buttload of potential and none of it was used.

While swatting will cause no harm to SCP-3343, it is theorized that the experience is personally humiliating for SCP-3343.

This broke the tone for me completely, and is what caused me to downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh… St Peter, the first pope… is a fruit… fly… Not feeling like we have the full article here. It just kind of feels like a snippet. Also, how did it assure the foundation that the Pietro events are harmless to humans if it can't speak?

I'm going to go on record and say that this one is a perfect candidate for an added interview. I feel that would add so much to this otherwise… dare I say it… Holey SCP… (I know, boo all you want, but the pun had to be made)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like you could have expanded more on the San Pietro events, specifically on the other words that manifested. Some other details on how a simple creature like a fruit fly could be "sinful" would be nice as well. Right now, there's nothing to this outside of Saint Peter, only a fruit fly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
no noticeable genetic differences between a tissue sample from SCP-3343 and an ordinary male fruit fly is present.

Should be 'difference', or 'are present'.

Though it has been SCP-3343 displays humanlike intelligence

Some part of a left-over sentence here?

enters what appears to be to be a meditative state for roughly 30 minutes

This is an approximation, so why do the Containment Procedures use 30 minutes as a maximum? Surely there's a reasonably good chance they'll have to cut it short?

Aside from these, it's a very good short-form scip, and I enjoyed it immensely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted. There's just simply not enough content here to hook me in, and the tone makes it out to be more of a -J than a mainlist SCP. Considering I didn't find it amusing and it doesn't succeed at eliciting any other emotion, there's nothing really here for me to chew on.

Minor note, but the third sentence in the description is grammatically incorrect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3343 lacks wings, and in their place, wears a cape-like white cotton robe of unknown origin. Attempts to remove the robe from SCP-3343 have been met with consistent failure.

My eyes skimmed over "-like white cotton robe" and I thought this was going to be a superhero fruit fly. Only slightly disappointed, but good article.

While swatting will cause no harm to SCP-3343, it is theorized that the experience is personally humiliating for SCP-3343.

Hehe, +1
